data.title: Vaso announces divestiture of VasoHealthcare for $0.8M
data.description:
• Vaso (OTCQX:VASO) announced on Wednesday that it had reached an agreement with Nano-X Imaging to sell one of its subsidiaries, VasoHealthcare IT.
  • The transaction will be considered up to $0.8M, with $0.2M payable at closing and up to $0.6M as an earnout based on the post-closing performance of the business.
  • The divestiture of VasoHealthcare IT will allow the company to focus on its core operations and competencies.
  • The company expects to use the proceeds from the sale to invest in its other business lines and for strategic initiatives.

data.title: NextTrip signs LOI and enters exclusive discussions to acquire GoUSA TV platform
data.description:
• NextTrip (NTRP) has signed a Letter of Intent (LOI) and entered exclusive discussions to acquire the GoUSA TV platform, content library, and related assets.
  • Following completion of definitive agreements and closing currently anticipated to occur prior to year-end, GoUSA TV is expected to relaunch as a distinct brand Powered by NextTrip, operating alongside JOURNY, Travel Magazine, and other NextTrip Media properties.
  • NTRP +1.96% after hours to $4.16.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Merck gets EU nod for subcutaneous version of Keytruda
data.description:
Merck (MRK) has received approval from the European Commission for a subcutaneous injectable version of Keytruda (pembrolizumab), its anti-PD-1 therapy, for all adult indications. 

The subcutaneous injection of Keytruda includes pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa. It has been authorized for all 33 indications for adult patients in Europe.

The availability of Keytruda in different EU countries will vary based on several factors, particularly the completion of national reimbursement processes, the company said.

data.title: Spain to probe Meta over privacy breach of Android users
data.description:
Spain will launch a probe into Meta Platforms (META) for the alleged privacy violation of millions of users who used the company's social media apps, according to an official statement from the prime minister's office on Wednesday.

Pedro Sánchez, the country's prime minister, said the social media and tech giant will have to testify and provide answers to the Spanish government after a research study revealed that "a hidden mechanism" allowed web tracking of Android device users (GOOG) (GOOGL) for nearly a year. 

Sánchez alleged that Meta collected web browser data of users through "silent spying" and linked it to their identity in Facebook and Instagram apps, even when browsing in incognito mode or on a VPN.

Spain believes Meta may have breached the EU bloc's GDPR rules, the Digital Markets Act, and the Digital Services Act. 

Sánchez also said Spain is taking measures to address issues like disinformation campaigns, protection of minors, combating hate speech, and social polarization in the digital space in the coming months.

data.title: Protara down after mid-stage trial data for lead asset
data.description:
Protara Therapeutics (TARA) slipped ~6% in the premarket on Wednesday after posting interim data from a mid-stage trial for its lead candidate, TARA-002, in children with lymphatic malformations, a rare congenital condition affecting lymphatic vessels.

Citing a Nov. 12 data cut from its Phase 2 STARBORN-1 trial, the New York-based biotech said that 80% of patients with macrocystic and mixed cystic lymphatic malformations achieved clinical success after completing the treatment.

The analysis was based on 12 patients who received more than one dose of TARA-002 as part of the single-arm, open-label study.

The company added that out of eight evaluable patients, seven achieved clinical success after one or two doses, while one who required all four doses indicated a complete response.

Regarding safety, Protara (TARA) noted that a majority of the adverse events were mild to moderate, and there were no serious adverse events linked to the cell-based therapy. However, one patient has discontinued the study due to a Grade 2 adverse event.

data.title: Mawson Infrastructure to enact 1-for-20 reverse stock split; shares down
data.description:
• Mawson Infrastructure (MIGI) board approved a 1-for-20 reverse stock split of its common stock, effective 5:00 p.m. ET on November 20, 2025.
  • MIGI shares will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis on Nasdaq on November 21, 2025.
  • Stockholders had previously authorized a reverse split within a 1-for-2 to 1-for-30 range at the October 15, 2025 annual meeting.
  • The split aims to raise the share price to regain compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid requirement.
  • MIGI shares fall 20% premarket.

data.title: Block to increase stock buyback program to $5B
data.description:
• Fintech firm Block (XYZ) will increase its stock buyback program to $5B during its investor day event on November 19, the company said on Wednesday.
  • It will also present the full-year 2026 financial guidance and a three-year financial outlook during the event.
  • XYZ +2.93% premarket to $59.33.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Williams-Sonoma beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; raises 2025 operating margin outlook
data.description:
• Williams-Sonoma press release (WSM): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.96 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $1.88B (+4.4% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Q3 comparable brand revenue +4.0%.
  • Merchandise inventories +9.6% to the third quarter LY to $1.5 billion, including incremental tariff costs and a strategic pull forward of receipts.
  • Maintained strong liquidity position of $885 million in cash and $316 million in operating cash flow, enabling the company to deliver returns to stockholders of $347 million through $267 million in stock repurchases and $80 million in dividends.
  • New stock repurchase authorization of $1 billion.
  • Raises 2025 operating margin outlook.

  • OUTLOOK

    • We are reiterating our fiscal 2025 net revenue guidance. We expect annual net revenues in the range of +0.5% to +3.5% inclusive of the impact from the 53rd week in fiscal 2024 (vs. estimated growth of 1.67% Y/Y), with comps in the range of +2.0% to +5.0%.
    • We are raising our guidance on operating margin for fiscal 2025. We now expect an operating margin between 17.8% to 18.1% (with the 53rd week contributing 20bps in fiscal 2024).
    • Our updated guidance includes (i) the new Section 232 tariffs on furniture, (ii) the revised additional tariffs on China of 20%, (iii) the tariffs on India of 50%, (iv) the tariffs on Vietnam of 20%, (v) average tariffs on the rest of the world of 18%, (vi) the steel and aluminum tariff of 50% and (vii) the copper tariff of 50%.
    • For fiscal 2025, we expect annual interest income to be approximately $35 million and our effective tax rate to be approximately 26.0%.
    • Fiscal 2025 is a 52-week year. Our financial statements will be prepared on a 52-week basis in fiscal 2025 versus 53-week basis in fiscal 2024. However, we will report comps on a 52-week versus 52-week comparable basis. All other year-over-year comparisons will be 52-weeks in fiscal 2025 versus 53-weeks in fiscal 2024.
    • Over the long term, we continue to expect mid-to-high single-digit annual net revenue growth with an operating margin in the mid-to-high teens.

data.title: Regeneron gets EU nod for expanded approval of Libtayo
data.description:
Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) has received approval from the European Commission for Libtayo (cemiplimab), to be used as an adjuvant treatment for adults with cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma ((CSCC)).

This new approval broadens the existing indication for Libtayo in the European Union, now including patients with advanced CSCC who face a significant risk of their disease returning.

The decision to approve Libtayo is backed by findings from the global Phase 3 C-POST trial, which compared the effectiveness of adjuvant Libtayo against a placebo in patients with CSCC who are at a high risk of recurrence following their surgical and radiation treatments.

data.title: Madison Pacific Properties Inc. reports results
data.description:
• Madison Pacific Properties Inc. press release (MPC:CA): 9M the Company is reporting a net income of $20.9 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $13.6 million); 

  • non-cash operating balances of $8.0 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $9.8 million); and income per share of $0.27 (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $0.22). Net income includes a net gain on the fair value adjustment on investment properties of approximately $18.6 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $7.8 million), losses on fair value adjustment on interest rate swaps of $1.1 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $2.7 million), interest expense of $11.7 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $9.5 million), interest income of $0.6 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $1.7 million), and equity losses of associate and joint ventures of $1.2 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: earnings of $0.8 million).

    As at September 30, 2025, the Company owns approximately $758 million in investment properties (December 31, 2024: $724 million).
